response.justification: While Bogdan brings 20 years of software development experience and strong leadership credentials, he is fundamentally misaligned for this C++ industrial-automation role. His last significant C++ work was 2014–2015; his entire career over the past decade has centered on web/JavaScript/Node.js development. He lacks demonstrated experience with message brokers, MQTT, secure SDL frameworks, IEC 62443 standards, or industrial-automation protocols—all core competencies for this position. His exceptional breadth makes him a strong candidate for generalist engineering leadership, but represents substantial risk for a specialized technical role requiring deep C++ and domain expertise.
strengths: Exceptional 20-year career spanning embedded, web, mobile, and desktop systems—demonstrates ability to acquire complex technical knowledge across domains
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, clean code) and mentorship with proven ability to lead engineering standards and establish quality practices
Holds BS Computer Science and has architected scalable systems handling millions of requests, demonstrating foundational engineering rigor and system-design capability
concerns: Critical C++ expertise gap: last substantive C++ work was 2014–2015 (11 years ago); entire recent career focused on JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js, not compiled systems or performance-critical environments
Zero demonstrated knowledge of industrial-automation domain, message brokers, MQTT, IEC 62443 standards, industrial cybersecurity, or relevant protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA)
No evidence of secure development lifecycle implementation, multi-threaded system design for high-performance production systems, or IoT/industrial applications beyond a volunteer GIS mapping project

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ expertise and domain discontinuity
Missing industrial automation and cybersecurity standards knowledge
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and proven leadership
